channels.name: ReligionForBreakfast
channels.notes: Andrew Mark Henry (PhD); the closest analog to our format - academic, non-devotional religion explainers; crossed 1M Jan 2026 (already tracked)

channels.name: Esoterica
channels.notes: Dr. Justin Sledge; lecture-style academic deep dives, hit 1M end of 2025; proves talking-head scholarship scales (already tracked)

channels.name: Let's Talk Religion
channels.notes: Filip Holm (MA religious studies); calm academic long-form on world religions incl. Christianity and Sufism; 1.05M as of May 2026

channels.name: Hochelaga
channels.notes: Tommie Trelawny; cinematic curiosity-driven storytelling about religion and the strange; hit 1M subs the week of July 7 2026

channels.name: UsefulCharts
channels.category: Religion/History explained via charts and timelines
channels.notes: Dr. Matt Baker (PhD Religious Studies); chart-driven biblical genealogies and religion family trees; strong product/merch flywheel

channels.name: Voices of the Past
channels.notes: History told through firsthand written accounts incl. early Christian and ancient religious texts; premium narration model

channels.name: Dan McClellan
channels.notes: Scholar with 1M+ on TikTok; YouTube is smaller but fast-growing shorts engine; subs approximate (Social Blade ~167K, undated)

channels.name: MythVision Podcast
channels.notes: Derek Lambert; books every major critical scholar (Ehrman, Tabor); owns the secular-scholarship interview lane; subs approximate

channels.name: Bart D. Ehrman
channels.category: Critical New Testament scholarship/Lectures and courses
channels.notes: The most famous NT scholar alive running his own channel + paid courses; 200K+ as of April 2026; funnel model worth studying

channels.name: Alex O'Connor (CosmicSkeptic)
channels.notes: Within Reason podcast; secular but scholarship-literate audience overlaps heavily with ours; 1.99M as of June 2026

channels.name: Fall of Civilizations
channels.notes: Paul Cooper; gold standard for 3-4 hour narrative documentary craft and production values we aspire to

channels.name: toldinstone
channels.notes: Dr. Garrett Ryan; scholarly antiquity explainers that frequently cover the Roman world of early Christianity

channels.name: History Time
channels.notes: Pete Kelly; feature-length ancient world docs incl. religious history; 1.25M in April 2025, subs approximate for 2026

channels.name: Cogito
channels.notes: History-of-religion series (History of Christianity, Judaism, Zoroastrianism) is a direct format competitor; subs approximate

channels.name: Overly Sarcastic Productions
channels.notes: Red's mythology series proves comparative myth has a huge young audience; pacing and personality lessons for us

channels.name: InspiringPhilosophy
channels.notes: Michael Jones; scholarship-flavored apologetics with animations; competes for the same 'thinking viewer' search traffic

channels.name: Redeemed Zoomer
channels.category: Denominational history/Theology explained for Gen Z
channels.notes: Richard Ackerman; maps, denomination explainers and church-history storytelling; 685K as of April 2026, growing steadily

channels.name: Wes Huff
channels.notes: Exploded after Joe Rogan appearance Jan 2025; makes manuscript scholarship mainstream; 986K as of July 2026

channels.name: BibleProject
channels.notes: The giant of Bible education on YouTube; nonprofit crowdfunded studio model and visual language worth studying

channels.name: The Chosen
channels.notes: Largest crowdfunded media project ever; narrative Jesus content at scale; subs approximate (sources vary 1.2-1.7M)

channels.name: Crecganford
channels.notes: Jon White; data-driven mythology phylogenetics (world's oldest myths); 220K as of Feb 2026 and growing in our exact lane

channels.name: Gnostic Informant
channels.notes: Neal Sendlak; edgy comparative-religion deep dives plus on-location filming; ~235-239K subs

channels.name: Centre Place
channels.notes: John Hamer's Toronto lecture series; long-form academic lectures with loyal audience; subs approximate

channels.name: Kipp Davis
channels.notes: DSS scholar who grew fast via viral critiques of pop-apologetics claims; subs approximate

channels.name: Mike Winger (BibleThinker)
channels.notes: Passed 1M in 2026; ultra-long-form single-host Bible study model; announced 3-month hiatus May 2026

channels.name: Kings and Generals
channels.category: Animated history documentaries/Military and religious history
channels.notes: 4.16M as of June 2026; factory production model; frequent series on Crusades, early Islam, Christianization

channels.name: Bishop Robert Barron
channels.category: Catholic commentary/Word on Fire media empire
channels.notes: Largest single-cleric channel; authority-driven talking head at scale; subs approximate (2026 trackers say 3.1M, passed 1M Feb 2024)

channels.name: Ascension Presents
channels.category: Catholic media/Fr. Mike Schmitz/Bible in a Year
channels.notes: Publisher-backed creator network; Bible in a Year podcast crossover shows power of daily-habit formats

channels.name: CrashCourse
channels.notes: 2024 Religions series with John Green mainstreamed academic religious studies; curriculum-series playbook; subs approximate

channels.name: Cliffe Knechtle (Give Me An Answer)
channels.notes: 940K subs; viral shorts machine from campus dialogues; format tangential to us but dominates the algorithm in this space

channels.name: Capturing Christianity
channels.notes: Cameron Bertuzzi; interview/debate hub bridging philosophy of religion and lay audiences; ~402-416K subs

channels.name: Paulogia
channels.notes: Point-by-point responses to apologists; the counter-programming side of our audience; subs approximate

channels.name: Genetically Modified Skeptic
channels.notes: Drew McCoy; empathetic atheist commentary, gained ~12K subs/30 days in 2026; subs approximate

channels.name: Truth Unites (Gavin Ortlund)
channels.notes: Scholar-pastor doing church-history storytelling with a calm tone close to ours; subs approximate

channels.name: The Ten Minute Bible Hour
channels.notes: Matt Whitman; curious non-dogmatic 'visit every denomination' format is a storytelling template; subs approximate

channels.name: The Histocrat
channels.notes: Multi-hour narrated deep dives incl. ancient religion topics; subs approximate

channels.name: Sam Aronow
channels.notes: Definitive Jewish-history channel, adjacent coverage of Second Temple era; 82K in 2024, subs approximate

channels.name: Digital Hammurabi
channels.notes: PhD couple (Josh Bowen, Megan Lewis) on ancient Near East context of the Bible; small but scholarly credible; subs approximate

channels.name: History Valley
channels.notes: Jacob Berman; prolific scholar-interview channel in the MythVision mold; subs approximate (trackers conflict)

channels.name: Blogging Theology
channels.notes: Paul Williams; hosts major biblical scholars from an interfaith angle, shows cross-faith demand for scholarship; subs approximate
